private_artifact_classes: P2 batch summaries, request metrics, system samples, and structured-error ledgers
P4 positional-schema batch summaries and request-level error metrics
twenty compact-schema P4 batch records containing eighty request measurements
12B automatic-placement and maximum-GPU P2 summaries plus one load read-back

parallelism.basis: Applied runtime parallelism and application concurrency match, and measured request-duration totals exceed batch wall time by approximately the admitted concurrency while each direct P2 pair has mean request latency close to pair wall time.

P1.detail: Sequential warmups establish single-request viability for the positional P4 workload, but the inspected closure artifacts do not contain a repeated, denominator-matched P1 throughput matrix.

P4_positional_const_schema.result: HTTP 400 before useful generation on all four model families
controls: reducing context did not repair the positional schema

latency_denominators.request_latency: One measured request; P4 means and ranges use 20 requests per model.
latency_denominators.batch_wall: Elapsed wall time for one concurrent measured batch; warmup excluded.
latency_denominators.end_to_end: Sequential warmup plus measured batch where retained by the runner.
latency_denominators.warning: Request latencies must not be added to batch wall time or compared as if they were serial throughput. No denominator-matched P1 baseline was retained for the repaired P4 repeat-five closure.

cache_claims.claim: The source application serializes lifecycle ownership and reuses a compatible loaded runtime.
cache_claims.basis: Pinned static source review plus deterministic owner-path tests.

cache_claims.claim: The source application sends cache_prompt=true on its LM Studio payload path.
cache_claims.basis: Pinned payload-builder review and deterministic payload tests.

cache_claims.claim: The source application creates a byte-stable LM Studio prefix or a cache-materialization barrier.
cache_claims.basis: The inspected builder ignores the provider-style cacheable-prefix seam; awaiting request one proves ordering only.

cache_claims.claim: E2B stateful execution is functionally usable in a small loaded-session probe.
cache_claims.basis: Root and branch requests completed with verified lifecycle cleanup.

cache_claims.claim: E2B stateful branches benefited from reuse.
cache_claims.basis: One run showed 4.3% lower total latency and another showed lower prompt-processing timing than full-prefix requests, without cached-token telemetry.

cache_claims.claim: 12B exact repeats benefited from session/runtime reuse.
cache_claims.basis: Follow-ups were much faster than the first request, but all outputs were empty length terminations and cached_tokens was unavailable.
Follow-ups were faster than the first request, but all outputs failed validity and no runtime cache counter was exposed.

cache_claims.claim: Physical KV reuse, cache hits, cache persistence, or avoided prefill occurred.
cache_claims.basis: No request-linked cache-hit flag, positive cached-token count, reused-token count, or physical KV trace was retained.

gpu.workload: P2 structured pair at 4096 maximum output tokens
limitations: No per-layer placement or physical VRAM telemetry was retained.
Completion-token totals differ, so wall-time difference is not a controlled placement-only speed comparison.
The load report did not show a larger model allocation under maximum-GPU placement.

recommendation.bounded_option: P4 only for compact generic schemas with strict post-generation identity/order validation and task-specific semantic review.
do_not_admit: maximum-GPU placement as a performance improvement
observability_gaps: request start and finish timestamps tied to worker slots
TTFT and prompt-processing timing for every parallel request
per-request raw or privacy-safe semantic-review artifact for P4
per-layer GPU placement, physical VRAM, utilization, and power telemetry

unresolved_evidence_gaps: The repaired P4 outputs were structurally validated but not retained or semantically reviewed.
The repaired P4 closure has no matched P1 baseline.
Physical cache reuse remains unproven for every model and route.
The automatic-versus-maximum-GPU comparison does not isolate placement from generated-token count.
No comparable cache/session A/B exists for 26B.
